title: 10 best herbal essential oils for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for oily hair are natural extracts used to control and balance excess oil production on the scalp. They help by reducing sebum secretion, preventing clogged pores, and soothing irritation. The best herbal essential oils for oily hair include Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Juniperus communis, Santalum album, Melissa officinalis, and Cinnamomum camphora. If left untreated, oily hair can lead to social anxiety, self-consciousness, and decreased self-esteem due to the constant struggle with greasiness, making everyday activities challenging and affecting overall well-being.

remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Help to combat scalp infections and reduce oil production by targeting the source of the problem, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with oily scalps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Soothe and calm irritated skin and scalp, reducing redness and inflammation caused by clogged pores and excess sebum, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Astringent properties: Help to balance the scalp's natural pH level, tightening the pores and reducing oil production, leaving hair looking cleaner and feeling lighter.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. It helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing the production of sebum and preventing clogged pores. The oil's ability to penetrate deep into the hair follicles and scalp also helps to break down excess oil and debris, leaving hair looking and feeling cleaner and healthier. Regular use can lead to a reduction in oily hair and a fresher, cleaner scalp overall.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps reduce sebum production and balances the scalp's natural oil production, leading to a decrease in oily hair.
Linalool: It has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help control scalp infections and inflammation, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.
Piperitone: It has antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to balance the scalp's natural oil production and reduce the appearance of oily hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are commonly used to produce Tea Tree essential oil, which is known for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help control oily hair and scalp conditions.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used to produce essential oil, which is rich in compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that help reduce oil production and prevent scalp irritation.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that can help reduce the oiliness of hair, while also soothing and calming the scalp to prevent conditions like dandruff and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature melaleuca alternifolia leaves and stems from the field when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the plant material in a stainless steel apparatus for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the condensed steam and separate it from the plant material using a separator or distillation apparatus.
Filter the essential oil to remove any impurities or plant residue using a filter paper or cartridge.
Store the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

Anti-inflammatory property: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can lead to oily hair by causing irritation and redness, while also soothing and calming the scalp.

Additionally, lavender oil's ability to stimulate blood circulation can promote healthy hair growth and reduce the appearance of oiliness, leaving hair looking cleaner and more balanced. This results in a healthier and more manageable scalp.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils for oily hair because they are rich in calming and antiseptic properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils for oily hair because they have astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil and purify the scalp.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a still using a ratio of 1:2 flowers to water.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillate and discard the solids.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove impurities.

Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 2 hours with a 10:1 water to herb ratio.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a separate container with a total volume of 10ml.
Filter the essential oil using a microfiber filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a healthy plant when they are at their peak fragrance.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c with 2kg of distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the distillate from the water and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered distillate in a clean glass bottle and let it mature for 24 hours before use.
Add a few drops of the matured thymus vulgaris essential oil to a carrier oil for topical use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of fresh leaves from the pelargonium graveolens plant when they reach full maturity after 6-8 months of growth.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100-110°c using a 1:4 leaf-to-water ratio for 3 hours to release essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation and transfer it to a clean container.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any remaining plant particles and improve its clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass container at room temperature for up to 1 year.

Collect the resulting essential oil mixture and separate it from the water layer via decantation and filter through cheesecloth for impurities removal.
Store the petroselinum crispum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding solids.
Transfer the resulting essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with oily hair because it contains calozone, a compound that has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to the scalp, it helps to balance the production of sebum, reducing excess oil and purifying the pores. This results in a cleaner, healthier-looking mane with less greasiness and more manageability. Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the pot marigold plant are known for their antiseptic properties, making them effective in controlling scalp infections that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers at a ratio of one part flower to two parts carrier oil.
Combine the dried calendula flowers with sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to infuse for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the resulting herbal essential oil in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves in early summer when they are still tender and have not yet turned brown.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth.
Allow the dried leaves to sit at room temperature for 7-10 days, stirring occasionally, to ensure thorough drying.
Once dry, grind the leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Store the powdered crataegus monogyna leaves in an airtight container to preserve their aroma and medicinal properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the scalp's sebum production. The oil also contains antifungal and antibacterial compounds that work together to combat dandruff and flakiness, while reducing the appearance of greasiness. Additionally, the oil's light and non-greasy texture allows it to be easily absorbed into the hair, leaving a fresh and clean scent without weighing down or building up on the scalp. Barks: High in tannins and phenolic acids, which help to tighten pores and balance skin pH, ideal for managing excess sebum and preventing clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a warm place for three days.
Blend one tablespoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with two tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the blend for four hours at room temperature then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the infused oil in a cool dark place for three weeks to allow the active compounds to mature.
Strain and filter the oil again before using it as a treatment for oily hair and scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with oily hair because it has natural sebum-regulating properties that help to balance the production of oil on the scalp. The antioxidants present in sesame oil also work to absorb excess moisture and purify the hair, leaving it feeling light and non-greasy. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sesamum indicum seeds, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides, weighing about 100 grams.
Dry the sesame seeds in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours, monitoring humidity levels.
Cold-press the dried sesame seeds using a hydraulic press or a screw-type extractor, extracting the oil without heat or chemicals, taking approximately 1 hour.
Filter the extracted sesamum indicum essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment, allowing it to settle for 2 hours.
Bottle the filtered sesamum indicum essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling and storing them in a cool dry place, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with oily hair because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to control sebum production. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves from sustainable sources weighing approximately 50 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours or until crispy.
Crush the dried leaves using a mortar and pestle into a fine powder measuring about 20 grams.
Steam distill the powdered syzygium aromaticum with 500 milliliters of pure water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters to remove impurities and residue.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal essential oils are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These skin conditions often share similarities with oily hair in terms of excess oil production, clogged pores, and irritation. Herbal essential oils like tea tree, lavender, and chamomile can help balance skin and scalp oils, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin and hair growth.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal essential oils that help with oily hair is a blend of tea tree oil, lavender oil, and lemon oil. Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp irritation and inflammation, while lavender oil soothes and calms the scalp, reducing oil production. Lemon oil, with its astringent properties, helps to balance the scalp's pH, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The oil's decongestant properties also help to unclog pores and improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce oil buildup and promote healthy hair growth. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce sebum production, which contributes to oily hair, by inhibiting the activity of sebaceous glands.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to dry out the scalp and reduce excessive oil secretion.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate scalp irritation and reduce the production of sebum, leading to oilier hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and astringent properties to help balance and control oil production in oily hair.
Flowers: Used for their calming and antiseptic properties to reduce stress and inflammation associated with oily hair, promoting a healthy scalp.
Stems: Used for their astringent and antiseptic properties to help regulate oil production and reduce dandruff and itchiness in oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal essential oil content and potency.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for at least 7 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a ratio of 1 part flowers to 3 parts water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation and store it in dark glass containers.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any residual particles and impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with oily hair because its antimicrobial and antifungal properties can help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production. remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce oil production in the hair by its astringent properties, which tighten the hair follicles and minimize the secretion of sebum.
Linalool: Exhibits antifungal properties that prevent the growth of microorganisms that can cause scalp infections, leading to excessive oil production and hair loss.
Terpinen-4-ol: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, killing bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to oil buildup and hair problems.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used to create Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils due to their high concentration of cineole, a primary active compound beneficial for oily hair.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to produce essential oils that help to balance and purify the scalp, reducing oiliness and dandruff.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils that are known for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to control scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-5 kilograms of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers from mature plants at the peak of potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material for 3-4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water through decantation.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its color and clarity.
Store the final product in dark-tinted glass bottles with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance and regulate oil production. Bornyl acetate: Has a decongestant effect, which helps to reduce the appearance of oily hair by unclogging pores and reducing the buildup of oils on the scalp.
Bornel: Possesses antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to eliminate bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to create essential oils due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which helps to balance and regulate oil production in the scalp.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at peak potency and immediately use a sharp knife to cut them into small pieces.
Steam distill the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a 1:3 ratio of leaves to water at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or a hydrosol collector to obtain 30 milliliters.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and improve its quality and clarity for 30 minutes.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a cool temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help balance the scalp's pH level and reduce oil production. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems from a field or garden in early morning when dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100-110°f for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oils and separate them from the water using a separatory funnel to obtain a clear oil.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid and place it in a cool dry storage area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with oily hair because its invigorating properties stimulate the scalp, helping to regulate oil production. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum oil quality.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves and flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage within 2 hours.
Steam distill the cleaned thymus vulgaris plant material in a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours.
Separate the distilled essential oil from the water using a condenser and collect the oil in a glass container within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle within 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis essential oils helps with oily hair because it has a natural astringent and antiseptic property that helps to balance the scalp's oil production. Distill the dried berries using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 80-100 degrees celsius.

Steam distill the plant material in a stainless steel distillation apparatus at 100c for 3 hours.
